zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Cokayne於大約1388年出生在英國貝德福爾考卡 哈特利時，她的父親John Cokayne30歲，母親Baroness Ida de Grey21歲。她於1410年之前與Sir Philip le Boteler結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1473年9月25日在英國英格蘭貝德福爾考卡 哈特利享年86歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Cokayne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cockayne
meanings: English (Midlands and Yorkshire): nickname for a dreamer, from Middle English cokayne ‘cloud-cuckooland’, the name of an imaginary paradise (Old French (pays de) cocaigne, from Middle Low German kōkenje, a diminutive of kōke ‘cake’, since in this land the houses were thought to be made of cake). It may also be a variant of Cocking or from the unflattering Middle English nickname cokin (Old French coquin) ‘disreputable person’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Cokayne was born about 1388, in Cockayne Hatley, Bedfordshire, England, her father, John Cokayne, was 30 and her mother, Baroness Ida de Grey, was 21. She married Sir Philip le Boteler before 1410. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 25 September 1473, in Cockayne Hatley, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 86, and was buried in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cokayneが約1388年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアコクアイン ハトリーで生まれた時，父John Cokayneは30歳，母Baroness Ida de Greyは21歳であった。1410年以前にSir Philip le Botelerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1473年9月25日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアコクアイン ハトリーで86歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.Woodhall, Watton, Hertfordshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 왓튼 앳 스톤
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cokayne(이)가 대략 1388년 영국 베드퍼드실르 콕아이인 하틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Cokayne(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Baroness Ida de Grey(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1410년 이전 Sir Philip le Boteler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1473년 9월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 콕아이인 하틀리에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에 묻혔다.

ru.Woodhall, Watton, Hertfordshire, England: ваттон ат стоун, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Cokayne родилась около 1388 г. в местности коккеин хатли, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Cokayne было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness Ida de Grey, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Philip le Boteler перед 1410 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 25 сентября 1473 г. в местности коккеин хатли, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.
